---
title: "Variable Witchgrass (Dichanthelium commutatum)"
slug: "dichanthelium-commutatum"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474875116/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Variable Witchgrass"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Poaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Poales", "Poaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474875116/medium.jpg" alt="Variable Witchgrass" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/ahevesh">
ahevesh</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/264444900">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Poaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=161782)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in woodlands, particularly in longleaf pine communities.

## Natural history notes

A perennial grass characterized by its branching stems and variable leaf size and shape. In Florida, it is found in a variety of habitats including woodlands, flatwoods, and disturbed areas. It grows during the warmer months and produces both open and more compact seed heads, contributing to groundcover in native plant communities.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/161782-Dichanthelium-commutatum)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/genus/27)
